topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The cardboard box landed on the worn welcome mat with a soft thud. Mark blinked slowly, the fluorescent light in the hallway seeming to hum louder than usual. He dragged his feet across the linoleum floor, the effort of movement feeling monumental. It was a package, addressed to the former occupant of Apartment 3B. He stared at the label, the ink blurring slightly under his tired gaze. A heavy sigh escaped him, a sound that seemed to vibrate in his chest. He shuffled toward the door, the weight of the box pulling him down with every step. He wondered if he had the energy to even lift it, let alone figure out where to put it.

[story 2]

I found the delivery waiting by the mailbox, a brown rectangle sitting incongruously against the pale siding of the building. My eyelids felt glued shut, heavy as lead weights. Lifting the package required a concerted effort; my arms felt strangely disconnected from my will. I leaned against the railing, trying to steady myself, but the world seemed to tilt slightly. Reading the faded handwriting on the return address took several attempts. Why was I doing this? The thought drifted through my mind like a slow, thick fog. Eventually, with a groan that sounded suspiciously like a plea for rest, I managed to haul the item inside, placing it gingerly on the nearest table.

[story 3]

The sound of the doorbell jolted me from a deep, unshakeable inertia. I shuffled to the entrance, my movements slow and deliberate, as if wading through deep water. There it was: a parcel addressed to someone who no longer lived here. My mind felt like thick syrup, each thought requiring significant concentration to form. I managed to open the door, the action itself demanding more energy than I possessed. Holding the package, I felt a profound sense of inertia, a desire to simply sink into the nearest chair and cease existing for a while. I managed a mumbled acknowledgment before slowly retreating back into the dim interior of my apartment.
